content: The line "All happy families are alike; each unhappy family is unhappy in its own way" was written by Leo Tolstoy. It is the opening sentence of his novel *Anna Karenina*.
raw_content: The line "All happy families are alike; each unhappy family is unhappy in its own way" was written by Leo Tolstoy. It is the opening sentence of his novel *Anna Karenina*.
reasoning: Okay, so I need to figure out who wrote the line, "All happy families are alike; each unhappy family is unhappy in its own way." Hmm, I remember reading something about this in a literature class. I think it's from a Russian novel, maybe from the 19th century. The name Tolstoy comes to mind. Wasn't there a famous novel that starts with this line? Oh right, "Anna Karenina"! So, Leo Tolstoy must be the author. I'm pretty sure that's the opening sentence of the book. Wait, but I should double-check to make sure I'm not confusing it with another work. I don't recall any other major novels starting with that exact quote. Yeah, I'm confident it's Tolstoy.
